4, 1952), containing information concerning the fluorides of aluminum, barium, beryllium, calcium, cesium, lead, lithium, magnesium, potassium, rubidium, sodium, strontium, and uranium.

The following conclusions are drawn from the observations: (1) in the case of the four halides of the same alkali metal the temperature coefficient $b$ of $X$ decreases continually with increasing at. wt. of the halogen atom; (2) at the same temperature the value of $X$ for the same halide of all the alkali metals decreases gradually with increasing at. wt. of the alkali metal; at the same temperature $X$ for the four halides of the same alkali metal gradually decreases with increasing at. wt. of the halogen atom.
